name: @platform/session-runtime
description: Session service: server-side session storage, token binding, session lifecycle, and claims propagation.

readme.summary: Session service: server-side session storage, token binding, session lifecycle, and claims propagation.
responsibilities: Store and retrieve session state backed by Redis
Bind authentication tokens to server-side session records
Propagate user claims and roles through the request context
Manage session expiry, rotation, and revocation
nonResponsibilities: Does not implement the authentication protocol (owned by security-auth)
Does not own the Redis connection or cluster configuration (adapters-redis)
Does not expose session data to client-side JavaScript
usage: Imported by the API runtime middleware pipeline to attach session context to requests
Application packages access user claims via the session context accessor
operationalNotes: Session expiry must be aligned with IdP token lifetimes
Redis key namespacing must isolate sessions from queue data
